summary.intent: Address three prior Codex review findings on the RangeCalendar component in fogodev/ars-ui PR #704, get CI green, and resolve all review threads to unblock merge.
summary.outcome: All three original review threads were fixed, resolved, and CI passed across the full matrix pipeline; Codex then posted three new P2 findings which were also fixed, validated locally, and committed — leaving PR #704 with green CI and no open review threads.

repo: ars-ui is a Rust/Leptos/Dioxus UI component library; core logic lives in crates/ars-components, adapter-specific specs in spec/leptos-components and spec/dioxus-components
Spec validation is machine-enforced via `cargo xtask spec validate` — spec markdown files are checked against implementation

CI pipeline is sequential/gated: snapshot check and formatting run first, then clippy, then a matrix fan-out of unit, integration, adapter, coverage, feature-flag (leptos/dioxus/subsystems), and release-verification jobs
Range constraint props (allow_single_date_range, min_range_days, max_range_days) must be declared in both leptos and dioxus adapter specs, not just the core component spec
Roving tabindex management must skip offset-aware outside-month duplicate cells to avoid multiple cells sharing tabindex=0 simultaneously

code.finding: Context construction: static unavailable dates were not being carried into Context or checked before the dynamic predicate — fixed by adding the static list to Context and short-circuiting before calling the dynamic predicate
Offset-aware cell trigger logic: outside-month duplicate cells were incorrectly included in roving focus management — fixed by skipping outside-month duplicates in roving focus assignment
Same-day range label logic: range-start and range-end label suffixes were not being built independently, causing same-day ranges to lose one endpoint state — fixed to preserve both
Adapter spec updated to declare range constraint props — previously missing, causing gap between core spec and adapter public surface
Same range constraint props (allow_single_date_range, min_range_days, max_range_days) added to match leptos adapter spec

workflow: GitHub GraphQL API mutations can programmatically resolve review threads, enabling scripted PR hygiene immediately after applying fixes
Triggering @codex review after resolving threads starts a new review cycle that may surface fresh findings — budget time for iterative fix→validate→review loops
Local pre-push checklist: cargo test, cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -D warnings, cargo xtask spec validate, cargo llvm-cov test
Co-authoring commits with Codex via Co-Authored-By trailer is a convention in this repo when Codex contributed to the fix
Polling gh pr checks on a gated CI pipeline requires patience; clippy alone can take several minutes before the matrix fan-out begins

friction: CI pipeline is strictly sequential/gated — formatting and clippy must fully pass before the matrix jobs start, making total wall-clock time to confirm all checks green very long
Codex posted three new P2 findings after the original three were resolved and CI went green, requiring a full additional fix-validate-commit cycle
Adapter specs (leptos, dioxus) are maintained separately from the core component spec and must be manually kept in sync — the gap was not caught until the second Codex review pass
open_items: Coverage and Release Verification lanes were still running when the second Codex review arrived — their final status was not confirmed in the transcript before the session ended
The adapter prop forwarding gap (range constraint props missing from leptos/dioxus specs) was described as a spec sync issue that could recur for future props added to the core spec
